content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

April 2, 1992

John M. Lane, Councilman  
1249 Riverside Drive  
Rio Dell, CA 95562

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. G-92-98

Dear Mr. Lane:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

The Commission provides advice to persons with duties and responsibilities under the Act and to their authorized representatives. The Commission does not provide advice to third parties about another person's duties or obligations under the Act unless the third party has been authorized to request advice by the person whose duties or obligations are in question. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329.) For this reason, we decline to provide you with the advice you have requested. I hope the following general information will be helpful.

Section 82013(a) provides that any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year for the purpose of supporting or opposing state or local candidates or measures is a "committee." Please refer to Manual D--Campaign Manual for Committees Primarily formed to Support or Oppose the Qualification, Passage, or Defeat of a Ballot Measure (pages 16-19) for information regarding the forms committees must file and when they are due.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Section 84215(e) requires committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose local measures to be voted upon in one city to file the original and one copy of their campaign statements with the clerk of the city.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
